reasoning: Only one scheduled FOMC meeting (June 17-18, 2025) remains before the July 1, 2025 deadline. The Fed left rates unchanged in January, March, and May 2025, and Chairman Powell emphasized a "no hurry" and "wait and see" approach in May, citing inflation risks and tariff uncertainty. Markets currently price a >60% probability of a pause in June, while multiple major investment banks (including Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and Macquarie) expect the first rate cut of 2025 to occur in July or later. Given the Fed's cautious stance and lack of signaling for an imminent policy change, an unscheduled cut or a surprise June cut is unlikely.
